behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file paths (SessionCapabilitiesMutator.java, SessionCapabilitiesMutatorTest.java), the method name apply(), the test method name shouldPropagateVncCapsWhenRequestHasNoBrowserName, the capability keys (se:vncEnabled, se:noVncPort), the required comment text ('Always propagate VNC capabilities from the stereotype'), and the reproduction scenario. no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction essentially describes the fix: move VNC propagation before the browserName check, and add a specific comment. While it doesn't say 'change line X', it strongly implies the exact structural change needed. However, the instruction describes the problem (VNC caps missing when no browserName) and expected behavior rather than explicitly stating 'move the browserName check after VNC propagation'. The comment requirement is very specific but could be seen as a documentation requirement

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_vnc_propagation_order_in_apply_method uses a specific regex to check that VNC handling code appears before the browserName early-return check, enforcing a particular code structure. An alternative valid fix (e.g., removing the early return entirely, or adding a separate VNC propagation block after the browserName check, or using a helper method) would fail this positional regex test. The test also requires the exact pattern 'Object vncEnabled = slotStereotype.getCapability(SE_VNC_ENABLED)'

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include both textual inspection (test_vnc_propagation_order_in_apply_method, test_vnc_propagation_comment_exists, test_new_test_method_exists) AND behavioral execution via bazel test (test_session_capabilities_mutator_test_suite, test_existing_vnc_tests_still_pass, test_build_source_module, test_default_slot_matcher_vnc). The behavioral tests actually compile and run the Java code. Not every test is text-only.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require: (1) a new test method with specific name and @Test annotation, (2) VNC handling code positioned before browserName check in the apply method, (3) a specific comment string. A stub/no-op implementation would not satisfy these - particularly the order check requires actual code restructuring, and the bazel test suite must pass meaning the Java test must actually verify VNC propagation behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors specified in instruction.md are tested: VNC caps propagation when no browserName (test_vnc_propagation_order_in_apply_method, test_session_capabilities_mutator_test_suite), the comment requirement (test_vnc_propagation_comment_exists), and the new test method (test_new_test_method_exists).
